updated: July 18, 2026, weekend sync. Quiet Saturday, nothing program critical changed since Friday. The one new thing is a DTE severe weather alert for this afternoon, wind gusts over 60 mph that could down trees and power lines across Southeast Michigan. No program runs on the weekend and Week 3 is indoors at the Branch, so the only thing to watch is Branch power holding for Monday, the way the July 6 storm knocked it out. Monday July 20 is still the big day with two things due. Email the one filled out Excel Payroll Transcript, named NAACP Hybrid Payroll #2 Transcript, to gdytpartnermessage@ConnectDetroit.org by 5:00 PM Monday, Tab A is each hybrid stipend youth's total benchmarks across July 6 to 10 and July 13 to 17, Tab B is the sign off, and only youth on the Approved for Placement list get entered. The YPC youth and chaperone field day registration for August 5 is also due Monday July 20, first come first served. Our own signed sign in and benchmark sheets are still required as backup and remaining pay is held until Connect Detroit has them, pay card pickup is Thursday July 23. DTE is still waiting on Jon Johnson to pick July 22 or July 29.
headline: Week 2, Financial Literacy, wrapped Friday, and Week 3, Entrepreneurship, starts Monday July 20. Huntington backed out of Banking 101 this year, so both the banking and credit sessions still need a partner. YNAB with Jermaine is set. DTE is answered: Jon Johnson got Wednesday July 22 or Wednesday July 29 at noon, and now you wait on him to pick. The YPC workshop is closed, Shuna wrote back this morning, so all that is left there is registering the youth and chaperones for the August 5 field day by Monday July 20. Wayne State has your answers, Jessica is building the July 28 schedule. CPR is approved and locked for Thursday July 30. Payroll 2 is the big one now and it has a Monday deadline: fill out the one customized Payroll Transcript Connect Detroit sent and email it to gdytpartnermessage@ConnectDetroit.org by 5:00 PM Monday July 20, that is what gets the 15 paid. Connect Detroit fixed the file, the corrected form is named NAACP Hybrid Payroll #2 Transcript so we have the right one. Your own signed sign in and benchmark sheets are still required as backup and remaining pay is held until Connect Detroit has them, pay card pickup is Thursday July 23, first pay July 31.

week1.tag: No power at the Branch after Friday's storm
week1.note: Canceled. Orientation moves to Wednesday. Everyone still gets paid for this day. Families were notified the night of July 6.

week1.tag: Done. Orientation plus U.S. DOL, in person 10 to 2
week1.note: Done. This was the first day. Orientation with Jermaine and Je'Tina, the U.S. Department of Labor (Mildred Kress) on wages and workplace rights, then AI Day Part 1 and Part 2.

AI Day Part 2, Resume From Zero

week1.tag: Done. Workplace Foundations · in person, 10 to 2
week1.note: Done. Day two, in person 10 to 2. Team-building and AI reflection, a built work-readiness session, then Workplace Ethics Court and Time Management.

sessions.activity: Slot a built session (Interview / Job Post / Decode)

week1.note: Done. Virtual and self-paced. Youth submitted their Capstone Kickoff on their own time. This closed out Week 1.

doNow.title: Get the YPC youth registration in before Monday
doNow.desc: The workshop half is closed. Jermaine passed on presenting Your Digital Footprint because he is out of the country August 4 to 11, and Shuna wrote back July 17 to accept it, so the $250 honorarium is off and that thread is done. What is left is the field day. The youth are still going Wednesday August 5 at Huntington Place, and he said he is registering the group and chaperones before Monday July 20. That registration is first come first served and Monday is the deadline, so confirm the org, youth and chaperone registration actually went in.

doNow.title: Cover the banking side of Week 2 (Huntington out)
doNow.desc: Courtney Elledge at Huntington wrote back and cannot do Banking 101 this year. She offered to send financial education and banking materials for the youth to use during the week. So now both Banking 101 and the credit session still need a plan. Take her materials, and either find a fast replacement or have Jermaine run Banking 101. Week 2 is already underway, so this needs a plan now.

doNow.title: Price CPR for staff and youth council
doNow.desc: Kamilia approved the CPR and First Aid cert for all 15 youth (Natosha Gurley, RN, $12 each, about $180). She now wants the total cost to also certify staff and some youth council members. Natosha is open to more people. Get a headcount and send Kamilia the number.

doNow.title: Confirm all 15 are on the final GDYT approved list
doNow.desc: Nicole Hill's July 14 update says the Youth Enrollment Tracker now holds the final Approved for Placement list, and only youth on it can work or be paid. Youth who worked before being approved cannot be paid for that time, and GDYT will not cover unapproved youth. Check the YET and confirm all 15 of ours show Approved for Placement. Also get the worksite ready for GDYT monitor visits over the next few weeks: original work permits on hand and a first aid kit.

doNow.title: Submit the Payroll 2 Transcript by 5 PM Monday
doNow.desc: Connect Detroit pivoted again July 17. myOneFlow will not be ready, so there is no action in the system. Instead you fill out the one customized Payroll Transcript they sent, an Excel form, and email it to gdytpartnermessage@ConnectDetroit.org by 5:00 PM Monday July 20. No other format is accepted and only one transcript per agency. On Tab A, our hybrid stipend youth, enter each youth's total benchmarks completed across July 6 to 10 and July 13 to 17, and put a 0 with a reason for anyone at zero. Tab B is the authorization sign off from whoever verified the benchmarks. The file is sorted, the first attachment was named for Mack Alive but Nicole sent a correction three minutes later and the right form is named NAACP Hybrid Payroll #2 Transcript. Separate from this, your own signed sign in and benchmark sheets are still required as backup, and remaining pay will not process until Connect Detroit has them, brought to pay card pickup Thursday July 23.

doNow.title: Get the 15 youth into the GDYT Empower App
doNow.desc: Kelsey at Connect Detroit sent the app sign-up reminder with a youth instructions deck. The app's modules also satisfy the GDYT financial literacy requirement, and it's separate from payroll. Connect Detroit will send weekly emails showing who still needs to register, so get everyone in early.

doNow.title: Lock the rest of Week 4
doNow.desc: CPR is locked for Thursday July 30 (approved). Still need Keosha's health day (7/29 or 7/30) and Zipporah's Financial Wellness date. Hold noon on July 29 until Jon Johnson answers, that is one of the two days you offered DTE.

doNow.title: Answer the two Week 3 virtual requests
doNow.desc: A brother and sister both emailed July 12 asking to work virtually July 20 to 29, and they are fully out July 21 to 24. That covers the in-person heart of Week 3 (Dream Car Reveal Tue 7/21, Entrepreneur Panel Thu 7/23). Reply to work out a virtual plan for those days so they stay on payroll and don't fall behind on the Capstone.

doNow.title: Confirm the two bounced families are set for the rest
doNow.desc: Two addresses bounced on the July 6 all-family email. Week 1 is done. Confirm both families by text or call that they have the schedule for the remaining weeks. One of the two also has a working gmail on the same thread.

waiting.name: Jessica Archer (Wayne State)
waiting.note: Answered July 15 at 5:14. You asked the group that morning and sent her the whole list: campus life, the law school and the professor named Ryan who got the biggest reaction, the BSMD eight year medical track, engineering and the new building, business and marketing, scholarships and internships, both asked unprompted, the Transfer Lounge, dorms, the Student Center, D9 and the black student orgs, the gym and the basketball courts, the game room, the Wright Museum, food, parking. Plus your two: what it takes to get in and what it costs, and a VP or dean on how to actually use a campus and its resources. You offered roster, forms and waivers same day. Now she builds the July 28 schedule and confirms lunch.

waiting.name: Jon Johnson (DTE)
waiting.note: Answered July 16 at 5:03 PM. Jermaine told him August 4 does not work because it is the Election Primary and the whole organization and the youth are on election work, then offered Wednesday July 22 or Wednesday July 29, both noon to 1:00, in person at the Branch, 15 young people ages 14 to 19, careers, networking, resumes, personal branding, whatever DTE wants to bring. He copied Mark, Monique, Je'Tina and Kamilia and offered to set the room. Now you wait on Jon to pick a day.

waiting.name: Zipporah Bolar
waiting.note: Checking her schedule for the Financial Wellness session (Week 2 or 4).

waiting.name: GDYT payroll + pay cards
waiting.note: Payroll 2 pivoted again July 17. myOneFlow will not be ready, so no one touches the system. Each partner fills out one customized Payroll Transcript, the Excel form Connect Detroit sends, and emails it to gdytpartnermessage@ConnectDetroit.org by 5:00 PM Monday July 20. No other format accepted, one per agency. Tab A is total benchmarks completed per youth for July 6 to 10 and July 13 to 17, Tab B is the authorization signature. The file is sorted, the first attachment was named for Mack Alive but Nicole corrected it and the right form is named NAACP Hybrid Payroll #2 Transcript. Backup documentation, your own signed sign in and benchmark sheets, is still required and remaining pay is held until Connect Detroit has it. Only youth on the final Approved for Placement list in the Youth Enrollment Tracker are paid, so confirm all 15 are on it (see Do Now), and GDYT monitors will visit to check safety, supervision, original work permits and a first aid kit. Our group is Payroll 2, first pay July 31, pay card pickup Thursday July 23 by Calendly appointment.

waiting.name: GDYT Empower App
waiting.note: Kelsey (Connect Detroit) sent app sign-up instructions Jul 6, with a youth how-to deck attached. Youth register in the Empower App; its modules cover the GDYT financial literacy requirement (separate from payroll). Connect Detroit sends weekly emails flagging who still needs to register.

waiting.name: Young Professionals Conference (Connect Detroit)
waiting.note: Wednesday August 5 at Huntington Place, 8:30 to 2:00, sponsored by DWIHN for the summer youth employment programs, free. The workshop is settled. Shuna asked July 15 for Your Digital Footprint again, two 45 minute breakouts, $250 honorarium, Jermaine declined July 16 because he is out of the country August 4 to 11, and she wrote back July 17 at 7:20 AM, thanks for letting me know, safe travels. Nothing owed there. What is left is the field day: register the org, youth and chaperones by Monday July 20, first come first served. Jermaine said July 16 the youth are going and he is registering the group and chaperones before Monday.

waiting.name: A participant asked for Wednesdays off
waiting.note: One of the young people emailed July 14 asking for Wednesdays off starting the week of July 22 to cover his other job. He works this full week out. Plan Wednesday coverage for the coming weeks without him. He is a participant, not staff.
